summaryText: Guest speakers Montell and Kristin Jordan shared a message titled 'The Miracle of Marriage,' framing marriage as a divine covenant where a man, a woman, and God become one. They explained that this oneness is not about losing individual identity but receiving an upgrade, reflecting the cooperative unity of the Trinity. The central theme was moving from a 'me' to a 'we' mindset, which requires a fundamental shift from selfishness to selflessness. To guide this transformation, they outlined five key stops on a metaphorical train journey: Submission, Sequence, being Sold Out, Sacrifice, and Serving. They taught that submission is a gift given to godly authority, not taken, and that men must first be under God's authority to lead effectively. Establishing a biblical sequence of priorities—God first, then spouse, children, church, friends, work, and hobbies—creates order and peace. The Jordans emphasized that marriage is a 100/100 partnership, not a 50/50 arrangement, where both spouses bring their whole selves, empowered by God who makes them whole. True love involves sacrifice, exchanging something of value for something of greater value, which is an act of worship. Finally, they championed a commitment to out-serving one another, stating they have never seen a marriage fail where both spouses are dedicated to this principle. The message concluded with an invitation for anyone to begin or renew a relationship with Jesus Christ, the ultimate source of completion and salvation.
mainPoints: Marriage is a divine covenant designed to reflect God's unity, requiring a shift from a 'me' to a 'we' mindset through selflessness.

A healthy marriage is built on five pillars: submitting to godly authority, prioritizing biblically (God first, spouse second), being 100% sold out, making sacrifices as worship, and committing to out-serve one another.
Only God can make an individual whole; spouses bring their complete, God-filled selves to create a powerful partnership that serves as a witness to others.

versesMentioned.text: For we are God’s handiwork, created in Christ Jesus to do good works, which God prepared in advance for us to do.

Then the Lord God made a woman from the rib he had taken out of the man, and he brought her to the man. The man said, 'This is now bone of my bones and flesh of my flesh; she shall be called ‘woman,’ for she was taken out of man.' That is why a man leaves his father and mother and is united to his wife, and they become one flesh. The man and his wife were both naked, and they felt no shame.
For I myself am a man under authority, with soldiers under me. I tell this one, ‘Go,’ and he goes; and that one, ‘Come,’ and he comes. I say to my servant, ‘Do this,’ and he does it.

Follow my example, as I follow the example of Christ. I praise you for remembering me in everything and for holding to the traditions just as I passed them on to you. But I want you to realize that the head of every man is Christ, and the head of the woman is man, and the head of Christ is God.
Therefore, I urge you, brothers and sisters, in view of God’s mercy, to offer your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and pleasing to God—this is your true and proper worship.
just as the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.

If you declare with your mouth, 'Jesus is Lord,' and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved. For it is with your heart that you believe and are justified, and it is with your mouth that you profess your faith and are saved.

Monday.scripture: Ephesians 2:10 (NIV) - 'For we are God’s handiwork, created in Christ Jesus to do good works...'
Monday.action: Write down one way your unique design blesses your spouse or key relationships.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you for making me your masterpiece. Help me see my value in You so I can bring my whole self to my relationships.

Tuesday.scripture: Luke 7:8 (NIV) - 'For I myself am a man under authority...'
Tuesday.action: Identify one area where you can practice submitting to God's authority today (e.g., in a decision, a attitude, or a relationship).
Tuesday.prayer: Jesus, I choose to come under Your authority today. Teach me to trust and follow Your lead in all things.

Wednesday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 11:3 (NIV) - '...the head of every man is Christ, and the head of the woman is man, and the head of Christ is God.'
Wednesday.action: Review your daily priorities. Make one intentional choice today to put God first and your spouse (or future spouse) second.
Wednesday.prayer: God, show me any area of my life that is out of Your sequence. Help me order my loves according to Your design.

Thursday.scripture: Romans 12:1 (NIV) - '...offer your bodies as a living sacrifice...this is your true and proper worship.'
Thursday.action: Perform one act of sacrifice for your spouse or a loved one—something that costs you time, comfort, or preference—as an offering to God.
Thursday.prayer: Father, I offer this sacrifice as worship to You. Let my actions demonstrate the value You place on this relationship.

Friday.scripture: Matthew 20:28 (NIV) - '...the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve...'
Friday.action: Find one specific, unexpected way to serve your spouse or a close friend today.
Friday.prayer: Jesus, give me Your servant's heart. Show me how I can actively love and serve others today.

Saturday.scripture: Genesis 2:24 (NIV) - 'That is why a man leaves his father and mother and is united to his wife, and they become one flesh.'
Saturday.action: Plan a 'date' or intentional connection time with your spouse or a family member to nurture your 'we' identity.
Saturday.prayer: God, thank you for the gift of relationship. Bless our time together and deepen our unity, reflecting Your love.
